scenario: Multiple hard lockups with MCE events
symptoms: NMI watchdog: Watchdog detected hard LOCKUP on cpu 32
Kernel panic - not syncing: Hard LOCKUP
NMI watchdog: Watchdog detected hard LOCKUP on cpu 62
NMI watchdog: BUG: soft lockup - CPU#23 stuck for 23s! [titanagent:2336749]
mce: [Hardware Error]: Machine check events logged
CMCI storm detected: switching to poll mode
INFO: NMI handler (perf_event_nmi_handler) took too long to run

workflow.thought: Check kernel log for lockup messages
workflow.observation: Hard LOCKUP on CPU 32 and 62, soft lockup on CPU 23

workflow.thought: Identify zone involved in CPU 32 lockup

workflow.observation: PCP structure at ffff88bddf31fdd8 with empty lists

workflow.action: list -H ffff88bddf31fd38 -o page.lru -s page.index | paste - - | wc -l

workflow.observation: CPU 8 runqueue at ffff88addfa1ac80 with tasks

workflow.thought: Check kernel log for all messages
workflow.observation: MCE events logged for 97 days before lockups
Significant delays on many CPUs up to 25 seconds

root_cause_analysis: A root cause is unknown at this time.
Multiple hard lockups occurred on multiple CPUs on which tasks were running different code paths that had nothing to do with each other.

Lots of MCE events were being logged and also hrtimer/perf interrupts were taking too long time to be processed during the time frame of the occurrence of the soft lockup followed by the multiple hard lockups.

It may be possible that some faulty hardware was causing some CPUs to be getting slow or stalled on which tasks were processing some jobs with zone->lock or rq->lock spinlocks being held hence the soft/hard lockups.
